entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A grain legume crop cultivated in the semi-arid tropics, crucial for the economic well-being of subsistence farmers.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The environmental region where pigeonpea is primarily cultivated.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The leading global producer of pigeonpea, accounting for over 75 per cent of world production.

entities.name: STERILITY MOSAIC DISEASE
entities.description: A viral disease of pigeonpea that acts as a major constraint to cultivation in the Indian subcontinent.

entities.name: PIGEONPEA STERILITY MOSAIC VIRUS
entities.description: The virus that causes sterility mosaic disease.

entities.name: ERIOPHYID MITE
entities.description: The vector, Aceria cajani, that transmits sterility mosaic disease.

entities.name: INDIAN SUBCONTINENT
entities.description: The geographic region where sterility mosaic disease is a major constraint to pigeonpea cultivation.

entities.name: YIELD LOSSES
entities.description: The reduction in crop productivity, potentially up to 100 per cent, caused by sterility mosaic disease.

entities.name: EMARAVIRUS
entities.description: A virus associated with the etiology of sterility mosaic disease.

entities.name: RAPID PHENOTYPING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Techniques used for the rapid assessment of disease and host-range.

entities.name: DISEASE MANAGEMENT STRATEGIES
entities.description: Approaches and methods used to control and manage sterility mosaic disease.
